ModMetaData.name: Tile Biome Editor
ModMetaData.author: James Malone
ModMetaData.description:
Select any tile on the world map and change its biome.
When you pick a landing site you're stuck with whatever biome the planet generated there. Tile Biome Editor adds a "Change biome..." button to the selected tile so you can set it to anything you like — turn a scorching desert into a temperate forest, a bog into tundra, whatever the colony you have in mind needs.
[HOW TO USE] - Select a tile on the world map. The button appears both on the "select landing site" screen and the in-game world map. - Click "Change biome..." and choose a biome from the list. The tile is repainted to match.
[WHAT IT CHANGES] Only the tile's biome. Landforms (peninsulas, islands, coasts), rivers, roads, and tile names are left intact — so you can re-biome a desert peninsula into a forest and it stays a peninsula.
[IMPORTANT — TIMING] The biome drives map generation, so change a tile BEFORE its map is generated. Editing a tile only affects maps generated afterward; it does not rewrite a map that already exists.
[COMPATIBILITY] - RimWorld 1.6 (Odyssey). - Requires Harmony (load it above this mod). - Safe to add to an existing save, and safe to remove: it only adds a UI button and stores no data of its own.
Source and issues: https://github.com/james-malone/boringbiome
